thread_tweets.text: @tulipking gonna be even funnier next Monday when they show they've sold another $500M-$1B of MSTR ATM
thread_tweets.full_text: @tulipking gonna be even funnier next Monday when they show they've sold another $500M-$1B of MSTR ATM

thread_tweets.text:
Yes - I read it as he wants the commons to believe that, but it's just hollow signaling.

1) Why would they buy back any commons anywhere near levels where they just sold billions of them? It'd have to be lower.

2) Read the language around the repurchase program... it doesn't give any specified date, it just says they have the option to do it for up to $1B at some unspecified point in the future. They just wanted the headlines.

3) His last post. "Strategy expects to remain disciplined in its use of $MSTR issuance, particularly when the stock trades at or near 1x mNAV." He's literally saying they will continue to sell MSTR for cash at or *near* 1 mNAV. That's the real headline. Anywhere *near*... aka above, at, or even possibly below he'll continue to sell as much as he can. There's a reason he didn't say an exact number.
thread_tweets.full_text:
Yes - I read it as he wants the commons to believe that, but it's just hollow signaling.

1) Why would they buy back any commons anywhere near levels where they just sold billions of them? It'd have to be lower.

2) Read the language around the repurchase program... it doesn't give any specified date, it just says they have the option to do it for up to $1B at some unspecified point in the future. They just wanted the headlines.

3) His last post. "Strategy expects to remain disciplined in its use of $MSTR issuance, particularly when the stock trades at or near 1x mNAV." He's literally saying they will continue to sell MSTR for cash at or *near* 1 mNAV. That's the real headline. Anywhere *near*... aka above, at, or even possibly below he'll continue to sell as much as he can. There's a reason he didn't say an exact number.

tweets.text:
For the sake of Bitcoin I still hope Strategy and Saylor are humbled here and now.

Imagine how much worse it would be years down the road if we bounced now and they were allowed to continue using STRC to buy the top and they accumulate a few more % of the BTC supply that way.

Avoiding something happening now without any substantial structural changes in the way they plan to operate is just kicking the can down the road.

thread_tweets.text:
And just announcing they’re willing to sell a bit of Bitcoin is nothing.

And their “equity buyback program” is pure virtue signaling.

What cash are they going to buy back the equity with?

They literally just sold $1.1B of MSTR but announced they’re doing a buyback program where they *can* eventually buy $1B of it back down the road?

And the post right after talked about how they’ll continue to use MSTR as a tool for raising capital *at or near* 1 mNAV. Aka they’re still going to continue to dump more of it here ATM!

And they *can* buy back $1B of prefs, which would reduce a little of the debt, but they still would have ~$15B left on the books and the dividend obligations would barely be reduced… not only that, but they’re increasing on a per debt basis by hiking the yield.

And they still have $6.7B of convertibles that need to be taken care of.

More of this mess needs to be cleaned up.

And their “equity buyback program” is pure virtue signaling.

What cash are they going to buy back the equity with?

They literally just sold $1.1B of MSTR but announced they’re doing a buyback program where they *can* eventually buy $1B of it back down the road?

And the post right after talked about how they’ll continue to use MSTR as a tool for raising capital *at or near* 1 mNAV. Aka they’re still going to continue to dump more of it here ATM!

And they *can* buy back $1B of prefs, which would reduce a little of the debt, but they still would have ~$15B left on the books and the dividend obligations would barely be reduced… not only that, but they’re increasing on a per debt basis by hiking the yield.

And they still have $6.7B of convertibles that need to be taken care of.

More of this mess needs to be cleaned up.

tweets.full_text:
For the sake of Bitcoin I still hope Strategy and Saylor are humbled here and now.

Imagine how much worse it would be years down the road if we bounced now and they were allowed to continue using STRC to buy the top and they accumulate a few more % of the BTC supply that way.

Avoiding something happening now without any substantial structural changes in the way they plan to operate is just kicking the can down the road.

And just announcing they’re willing to sell a bit of Bitcoin is nothing.

And their “equity buyback program” is pure virtue signaling.

What cash are they going to buy back the equity with?

They literally just sold $1.1B of MSTR but announced they’re doing a buyback program where they *can* eventually buy $1B of it back down the road?

And the post right after talked about how they’ll continue to use MSTR as a tool for raising capital *at or near* 1 mNAV. Aka they’re still going to continue to dump more of it here ATM!

And they *can* buy back $1B of prefs, which would reduce a little of the debt, but they still would have ~$15B left on the books and the dividend obligations would barely be reduced… not only that, but they’re increasing on a per debt basis by hiking the yield.

And they still have $6.7B of convertibles that need to be taken care of.

More of this mess needs to be cleaned up.

thread_tweets.full_text: $STRC dividend rate has been increased by 50 bps to 12.00%, effective for record dates in July, 2026. We will continue to evaluate the rate monthly. Our corporate objective is for $STRC to trade over time at $99-$100.

thread_tweets.text: Strategy has increased its USD Reserve to $2.55B, representing 17.4 months of dividend coverage. The USD Reserve may be used only for dividends and interest expense, and will be maintained at a minimum of 12 months.
thread_tweets.full_text: Strategy has increased its USD Reserve to $2.55B, representing 17.4 months of dividend coverage. The USD Reserve may be used only for dividends and interest expense, and will be maintained at a minimum of 12 months.

thread_tweets.text: Strategy announces a Digital Credit Capital Framework designed to strengthen Digital Credit, enhance liquidity, preserve long-term Bitcoin exposure, and support long-term value creation. $MSTR $STRC https://t.co/AUoUCtem53
thread_tweets.full_text: Strategy announces a Digital Credit Capital Framework designed to strengthen Digital Credit, enhance liquidity, preserve long-term Bitcoin exposure, and support long-term value creation. $MSTR $STRC https://t.co/AUoUCtem53

thread_tweets.text:
Strategy has established a $BTC Monetization Program under which we may sell BTC to fund our:
- USD Reserve ($1.25B cap)
- dividends and interest expense
- repurchases of our Digital Credit securities and $MSTR under our repurchase programs
thread_tweets.full_text:
Strategy has established a $BTC Monetization Program under which we may sell BTC to fund our:
- USD Reserve ($1.25B cap)
- dividends and interest expense
- repurchases of our Digital Credit securities and $MSTR under our repurchase programs

thread_tweets.text: Strategy has established repurchase programs for up to $1.0B of our Digital Credit securities and up to $1.0B of $MSTR. This will create flexibility to accretively buyback securities during market dislocations. Repurchases will not be funded from the USD Reserve.
thread_tweets.full_text: Strategy has established repurchase programs for up to $1.0B of our Digital Credit securities and up to $1.0B of $MSTR. This will create flexibility to accretively buyback securities during market dislocations. Repurchases will not be funded from the USD Reserve.

thread_tweets.text: With $2.55B of USD Reserve and $1.25B of BTC monetization capacity for reserve-building, Strategy has $3.80B of dividend coverage, representing 25.9 months.
thread_tweets.full_text: With $2.55B of USD Reserve and $1.25B of BTC monetization capacity for reserve-building, Strategy has $3.80B of dividend coverage, representing 25.9 months.

thread_tweets.text: Strategy expects to remain disciplined in its use of $MSTR issuance, particularly when the stock trades at or near 1x mNAV.
thread_tweets.full_text: Strategy expects to remain disciplined in its use of $MSTR issuance, particularly when the stock trades at or near 1x mNAV.

tweets.full_text:
$STRC dividend rate has been increased by 50 bps to 12.00%, effective for record dates in July, 2026. We will continue to evaluate the rate monthly. Our corporate objective is for $STRC to trade over time at $99-$100.

Strategy has increased its USD Reserve to $2.55B, representing 17.4 months of dividend coverage. The USD Reserve may be used only for dividends and interest expense, and will be maintained at a minimum of 12 months.

Strategy announces a Digital Credit Capital Framework designed to strengthen Digital Credit, enhance liquidity, preserve long-term Bitcoin exposure, and support long-term value creation. $MSTR $STRC https://t.co/AUoUCtem53

Strategy has established a $BTC Monetization Program under which we may sell BTC to fund our:
- USD Reserve ($1.25B cap)
- dividends and interest expense
- repurchases of our Digital Credit securities and $MSTR under our repurchase programs

Strategy has established repurchase programs for up to $1.0B of our Digital Credit securities and up to $1.0B of $MSTR. This will create flexibility to accretively buyback securities during market dislocations. Repurchases will not be funded from the USD Reserve.

With $2.55B of USD Reserve and $1.25B of BTC monetization capacity for reserve-building, Strategy has $3.80B of dividend coverage, representing 25.9 months.

Strategy expects to remain disciplined in its use of $MSTR issuance, particularly when the stock trades at or near 1x mNAV.

tweets.text:
more on japan vs brazil

obviously 2 very different system clashing, japan, no superstars, but low key as a whole unit, they are the best team in the world cup so far, but thats just cat opinion

for brazil, obviously not as systematic, some of the players up front have better chemistry than rest of the team's combinations, the brazil team is obviously mogs if you compare players 1 by 1

cat think very waste of time trying to overly predict this game, variance is large, the odds are probably roughly correct, brazil more likely

therefore gun to head tho for me, i dont have opinions on who wins, but on other matter in this game, imo it ends at 90, there wont be extra time, i also think if japan is to win at all, it is likely they win by a larger margin expected

therefore gun to head tho for me, i dont have opinions on who wins, but on other matter in this game, imo it ends at 90, there wont be extra time, i also think if japan is to win at all, it is likely they win by a larger margin expected
